options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: victory_goal_logic
options.description:
Which victory should be achievable before the game is considered beatable?

Note that this solely affects progression logic. You can still beat the game earlier than expected or pursue a
different victory if you so choose.

Domination and Time victories are not included here as Domination can be achieved at any moment and Time has no
requirements.

options.name: era_goal_logic
options.description:
Which era must be unlocked before the game is considered beatable?

This is in addition to the value given for "Victory Goal Logic".
Similarly, this solely affects progression logic.

options.name: embarking_goal_logic
options.description:
Add Optics and Astronomy as requirements before the game is considered beatable.

Similarly to other goal logic options, this solely affects progression logic.

options.name: policy_cost_modifier
options.description: Multiplies the base cost of adopting social policies with the given percentage.

options.name: tech_cost_modifier
options.description: Multiplies the base cost of researching technologies with the given percentage.

options.name: progressive_techs
options.description:
Use progressive technologies.

Progressive technologies divides all 79 technologies (excl. Agriculture) into 11 categories.
This makes the unlocking of technologies feel less chaotic and more streamlined compared to true random
technologies. It also makes it far less likely to not have important early-era buildings in the mid to late game.

options.name: item_hints
options.description:
Enable item hints for all locations.

Options are:
- full: Locations state their exact item.
- classification: Locations state the classification of their item (progression; useful; filler; trap).
- none: Locations do not state their item.

options.name: disguise_traps
options.description:
Item classification hints will classify traps as progression items.

Keep in mind that if item hints are set to 'full', the name of the trap is still visible and could potentially spoil
you of the true nature of the item.

Has no effect if item hints are set to 'none'.

options.name: satellites_meets_all
options.description: Receiving the Satellites technology will also meet all civilizations and city-states.

options.name: building_sanity
options.description:
Add most standard buildings to the location pool.

This adds 53 buildings to the location pool in total.

Buildings that will not be added:
- Palace
- Courthouse
- Any religious building (Cathedral; Monastery; Mosque; and Pagoda)
- Any civilization-unique building (they count as the building they replace instead)
- Any National Wonder (see National Wonder Sanity instead)
- Any World Wonder (see World Wonder Sanity instead)

options.name: national_wonder_sanity
options.description:
Add all national wonders to the location pool.

This adds the 11 national wonders and the 3 guilds to the location pool.

options.name: world_wonder_sanity
options.description:
Add all world wonders to the location pool.

This adds the 47 world wonders to the location pool, including the 3 ideology world wonders.

WARNING: This option is NOT suitable for syncs.

options.name: enable_traps
options.description: Add traps to the filler item pool.

options.name: trap_blacklist
options.description:
Blacklist the given traps from being included in the filler item pool.

Has no effect if traps are not enabled.

Valid keys are:
Trap - All City Population -1
Trap - All City Population -2
Trap - Culture
Trap - Denounce
Trap - Faith
Trap - Gold
Trap - Major Culture
Trap - Major Faith
Trap - Major Gold
Trap - Minor Culture
Trap - Minor Faith
Trap - Minor Gold
Trap - War

options.name: trap_filler_chance
options.description:
The percent chance for a filler item to be a trap instead.

Has no effect if traps are not enabled.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.
options: World Wonder - Leaning Tower of Pisa
World Wonder - Great Mosque of Djenne

options.name: exclude_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an important item.
options: World Wonder - Leaning Tower of Pisa
World Wonder - Great Mosque of Djenne

options.name: priority_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an unimportant item.
options: World Wonder - Leaning Tower of Pisa
World Wonder - Great Mosque of Djenne

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.
